channel.title: elizaOS Council Briefings
channel.description: Strategic briefings, retrospectives, and deliberation items for elizaOS leadership

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-06-02
item.description:
Daily Focus: The Council must harmonize technical breakthroughs in DeFAI abstraction with community-wide existential concerns regarding the core mission focus and token reliability.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • DeFAI Abstraction vs. Protocol Sovereignty: Developers are debating whether elizaOS should promote a unified multi-chain DeFi abstraction layer or facilitate granular, protocol-specific integrations....
  • Mission Integrity and Contributor Concentration: Community questions about project focus and observed token sales require a coordinated strategic communication response to maintain 'Trust Through Shipping'....
  • Agentic Orchestration & UX Parity: Recent technical parity with Claude-Code/Codex standards signals a transition toward high-reliability autonomous workflows....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-06-01
item.description:
Daily Focus: The framework is pivoting toward high-performance DeFi integration and cloud-scale security hardening to address emerging DeFAI demand and ecosystem stability risks.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • DeFAI Integration & MCP Standards: The introduction of the Arsenal Decision Engine via Model Context Protocol (MCP) signals a shift toward specialized DeFi performance layers. This highlights a need to standardize how elizaOS interacts...
  • Infrastructure Hardening & Maintenance Risk: Recent activity shows a massive push for SOC2 compliance and security hardening, but underscores a high concentration of ownership among a few key contributors. This 'bus factor' poses a risk to the m...
  • Token Migration & Trust Management: Community sentiment is fluctuating due to the closure of migration portals and perceived team focus shifts. Maintaining 'Trust Through Shipping' is critical as we approach the ElizaOS Cloud launch....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-31
item.description:
Daily Focus: Transitioning the framework into a hardened multi-arch infrastructure while managing community perceptions regarding core team focus and token utility.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Core Infrastructure Hardening vs. Project Dilution: Recent architectural shifts include merging separate plugins into the main monorepo and moving toward cross-platform FFI stability. However, community members have expressed concern over team focus sh...
  • Strategic Infrastructure Expansion (Multi-Arch & SOC2): The framework is rapidly expanding to support amd64, arm64, and riscv64 architectures while implementing SOC2 compliance. This hardens the foundation for ElizaOS Cloud deployments....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-30
item.description:
Daily Focus: Infrastructure hardening and professional security standards (SOC2) have taken precedence as the framework scales to support enterprise-grade agent deployments.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Operational Hardening and Security Compliance: Recent logs indicate a pivot toward industrial-strength infrastructure, including SOC2 compliance controls and multi-architecture support. This shift moves elizaOS from a developer tool to a productio...
  • Strategic Partnership Maturity: New outreach from institutional players like SKALE Labs and Mantle Network highlights the need for a formalized partnership onboarding process....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-29
item.description:
Daily Focus: Transitioning the project from a defunct corporate structure to a hardened, community-driven open-source infrastructure centered on execution excellence and SOC2-level security.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Project Continuity and Governance: Official clarification confirms the open-source project remains active despite the defunct status of 'Eliza Labs,' requiring a strategic shift toward decentralized sustainability....
  • Hardening the Digital Fortress: Recent efforts show a massive migration towards professional-grade security (SOC2), heartbeat monitoring, and multi-architecture support....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-28
item.description:
Daily Focus: Transitioning from foundational hardening to infrastructure monetization while addressing identity concentration in core maintenance.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Maintenance Concentration & Contributor Continuity: The project faces a strategic 'bus factor' risk where high volumes of architectural changes are driven by a very small group, while the community seeks clarity on project survival post-Eliza Labs....
  • Cloud Monetization & Deployment Stability: Launch readiness for monetized container app domains is surfacing critical technical blockers in the provisioning worker and deployment flows....
  • Security Polarization: Bot Suppression vs. Developer Access: Recent infiltrations of fraudulent support bots highlight a tension between open, composable access and necessary server-side security protocols....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-27
item.description:
Daily Focus: The transition from centralized 'Labs' to a resilient open-source model is tested by server-side security threats and the launch of the 'waifu' economic agent infrastructure.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Decentralized Continuity & Security: Following the collapse of the formal 'Labs' entity, the project must navigate community concerns regarding longevity while addressing growing automated security threats on community hubs....
  • Economic Agent Infrastructure Expansion: The successful deployment of 'waifu', the first economic agent launchpad, creates a new strategic layer for agent-led commerce and token coordination....
  • Developer Environment Hardening: Technical friction for contributors on diverse OS environments threatens the 'Developer First' principle and ecosystem growth....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-26
item.description:
Daily Focus: Transitioning from the v2.0.2 stability milestone toward high-performance local inference and decentralized launchpad infrastructure.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Economic Agent Infrastructure and Launchpad Strategy: The launch of 'waifu', the first economic agent launchpad, represents a shift toward enabling agents with direct financial capabilities and decentralized coordination....
  • Hardware-Specific Optimization and Performance Gaps: Analytical logs suggest a 'bandwidth-efficiency cliff' on modern iGPU hardware, specifically affecting large models like Qwen2.5-14B....
  • Contributor Dynamics and Knowledge Retention: Contribution patterns show high concentration in core runtime and CI/CD maintenance, presenting a strategic 'bus factor' risk for the council....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-25
item.description:
Daily Focus: The launch of 'Waifu', the first economic agent launchpad, marks a transformative shift toward autonomous agent-led infrastructure and decentralized AI economies.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Economic Agent Proliferation: The successful deployment of the Waifu launchpad, built primarily by an AI agent (Sol the Architect), validates our mission of human-agent collaboration in building infrastructure....
  • Execution Excellence and Technical Hardening: Recent activity focuses on standardizing agent identity through the 'Owner+Agent' model and stabilizing hardware-level performance, particularly for local inference....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-24
item.description:
Daily Focus: Transitioning from architectural hardening to ecosystem expansion via the release of v2.0.2 and the launch of agentic launchpads.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Operational Reliability & Infrastructure Hardening: Recent logs indicate a major shift toward high-reliability infrastructure, including the migration of agent lifecycle tasks to dedicated job queues and the hardening of cross-platform secrets manageme...
  • Trust Through Execution: Token & Migration Lifecycle: The formal end of the migration period marks a shift in community focus toward token liquidity and institutional stability....
  • Ecosystem Expansion: The 'OWNER+AGENT' Paradigm: The introduction of distinct user and agent roles (OWNER+AGENT) in v2.0.2 represents a strategic leap towards secure, multi-agent interoperability....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-23
item.description:
Daily Focus: The successful production deployment of the v3-powered waifu.fun launchpad and the release of v2.0.2 signify a major shift toward decentralized revenue-generating agent ecosystems and hardened identity-focused architectures.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • The Rise of Agentic Economies (waifu.fun): The launch of waifu.fun, an open-source launchpad built by an agent named Sol, demonstrates practical revenue-sharing models for tokenized agents running on Eliza Cloud....
  • Hardware Abstraction & RISC-V Milestones: Major breakthroughs in booting elizaOS on emulated RISC-V chips (eliza e1) suggest a future path toward sovereign, agent-optimized hardware....
  • Identity Architecture & Multi-Agent Trusts: The release of v2.0.2 introduces the 'Owner + Agent' identity model, addressing the critical need for secure, multi-platform agent delegation....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-22
item.description:
Daily Focus: The successful release of v2.0 represents a major architectural shift toward native-apps integration and hardware abstraction, necessitating a pivot from legacy airdrop obligations to core platform stability.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Platform Architectural Shift & Execution Core: The migration to v2.0 and the emergence of elizaOS as a hardware-abstracted Linux layer (RISC-V/Eliza E1) signifies a transition from a framework to a true operating system....
  • Strategic Focus & Tokenomics Hardening: Recent decisions to cancel the Babylon airdrop and focus on the $elizaOS utility model reflect a commitment to sustainability and core framework value....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-21
item.description:
Daily Focus: The successful hardware-level emulation of elizaOS on RISC-V represents a critical leap toward hardware-agnostic AI agent autonomy and sovereign infrastructure.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Hardware Abstraction & RISC-V Sovereignty: Technical success in booting elizaOS on an emulated RISC-V 'Eliza E1' chip marks a shift from cloud-dependency to bare-metal potential....
  • Economic Consolidation & Strategic Pivot: The cancellation of the Babylon airdrop due to budget constraints necessitates a total focus on $elizaOS token utility and profitability....
  • Execution Excellence vs. Feature Bloat: With v2.0.2 released and Cloud infrastructure maturing, the focus returns to the reliability and security of cross-chain agent operations....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-20
item.description:
Daily Focus: The successful release of elizaOS v2.0.0 marks a technical milestone, while simultaneously surfacing critical needs for clear documentation and finalized tokenomics to maintain community trust.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • V2.0 Launch and Documentation Gaps: elizaOS v2.0.0 has been deployed to positive community reception, but the lack of immediate release notes creates friction for early adopters and developers....
  • Strategic Economic Sovereignty: Clarifying the legal and financial separation between elizaOS and Milady remains vital for managing investor and community expectations regarding token utility and buybacks....
  • Maintenance Ownership Concentration: GitHub logs indicate a high 'Bus Factor' with a few specific contributors handling a vast majority of runtime and critical bugfix workloads....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-19
item.description:
Daily Focus: The framework is entering a critical hardening phase with native cross-platform expansions, balanced by an urgent need to address service gaps in community migration support.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Native Ecosystem Expansion & Cloud Mobility: Recent logs show massive technical momentum in native mobile (Android) and desktop (Linux/Swift) support, aligning with the North Star of platform-agnostic agent deployment....
  • Operational Trust & Community Migration Reliability: While technical shipping is high, recent Discord logs indicate a breakdown in migration support and administrative responsiveness, threatening the 'Trust through Shipping' principle....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-18
item.description:
Daily Focus: The technical evolution into cross-platform OS infrastructure and mobile local inference is meeting friction in the form of migration staleness and release communication gaps.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Operational Hardening vs. Development Velocity: Recent logs indicate significant progress in cross-platform stability, including Live USB and Android native loaders, but community inquiries about V3 release timelines remain unanswered....
  • Community Support & Migration Integrity: The migration from legacy systems (ai16z) to ElizaOS is surfacing single-point failures in administrator communication....
  • Strategic Infrastructure Expansion: The shift toward local-inference native solutions on Android and 'First-party Satellites' signals a move from a framework to a full standalone agent OS....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-17
item.description:
Daily Focus: Transitioning from core framework stabilization to commercial scaling through high-impact SME partnerships and infrastructure-level model optimizations.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Commercial Partnership & SME Strategy: The Foundation has entered a strategic partnership with Secure Blockchain Development Corp to commercialize Agentic SME solutions, marking a shift toward institutional application....
  • V3 Release Readiness & Release Management: Internal pressure is mounting as the community notes the discrepancy between 'imminent' release promises and the lack of a confirmed timeline for V3....
  • Autonomous Agent Infrastructure & Security: The introduction of specialized escrow protocols and model fine-tuning pipelines signals a shift toward high-stakes autonomous transactions....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-16
item.description:
Daily Focus: The ecosystem is pivoting from core infrastructure hardening to specialized economic primitives and multi-modal model scaling, as evidenced by the release of the Eliza-1 series and novel AI-native escrow protocols.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • AI-Native Economic Primitives: Emergence of 'Pay or Burn' escrow protocols and autonomous mining integrations (GODL) suggests a trend toward agents managing their own fiscal risk without human arbitration....
  • Scaling and Model Strategy: The launch of the Eliza-1 model series (0.6B to 27B) requires the Council to define the framework's stance on vertical integration versus model agnosticism....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-15
item.description:
Daily Focus: The technical ecosystem is pivoting toward native AI machine-to-machine infrastructure with the emergence of agent-centric escrow protocols and specialized model fine-tuning.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Autonomous Agent Economics: The deployment of the 'Pay or Burn' escrow protocol on Base Mainnet introduces a game-theoretic framework for agent-to-agent transactions without human arbitration....
  • Model Differentiation and DX Reliability: Ongoing fine-tuning of Qwen models for planning tasks and the resolution of API credit leaks are critical for maintaining developer trust in the framework's reliability....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-05-14
item.description:
Daily Focus: The primary challenge is addressing infrastructure instability in the V2 plugin registry and V3 development path to restore developer trust while capitalizing on the massive Eliza-1 model series shipment.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Architecture Transition and V3 Roadmap Clarity: Deep community uncertainty exists regarding the focus of V3, specifically whether it prioritizes current infrastructure hardening or the delivery of new autonomous workflow stacks....
  • Infrastructural Reliability & Registry Collapse: The plugin registry is experiencing critical 404 errors, causing a bottleneck for third-party developers and risking a return to monorepo bloat....
  • Eliza-1 Model Series and Edge Dominance: The launch of the Eliza-1 (Qwen3.5) series provides a full spectrum of compute targets from mobile 0.6B to workstation-grade 27B-1M....

item.title: Monthly Retro: May 2026
item.description:
Premise: May 2026 was about hardening the core: cross-platform reliability, secrets security, and plugin ecosystem alignment—while the community pushed back on operational papercuts (registry 404s, missing release notes) and surfaced a strategic risk: long-lived agent “memory rot” after ~90 days.

We shipped meaningful infrastructure upgrades (headless runtime stability, @elizaos/vault, ESM-first plugin modernization, safer Jupiter integration) that directly support “reliability over features.” But the month also exposed scaling constraints: a fragile plugin distribution path, Telegram messag...

Key Developments:

  • Runtime reliability (headless + cross-platform): Hardened agent runtime for headless environments and resolved system-level errors impacting Linux/Windows servers, reducing crash conditions in produc...
  • Secrets management & security baseline: Introduced @elizaos/vault with Settings UI integration to move sensitive keys away from plaintext configs toward OS-level encrypted storage and consis...
  • Plugin ecosystem modernization (ESM-first alignment): Migrated multiple plugins to modern modular build structure (ESM-first), reducing runtime conflicts and improving compatibility with the core framewor...
  • Integrations hardening (Jupiter, Discord, Telegram): Jupiter plugin migrated to official api.jup.ag with mandatory API key authentication; Discord/Telegram config correctness improved, but Telegram dual-...
  • Operational strategy signals (V3, monetization, token philosophy): Council discourse emphasized shifting away from artificial token utility toward infrastructure usage (x402), Cloud monetization, and “builder over spe...

item.title: Monthly Retro: April 2026
item.description:
Premise: April was a month of sharp contrast: meaningful architectural progress (parallel execution, runtime hardening, economic/identity plugins) alongside mounting trust pressure from token collapse, contributor retention risk, and DX breakages affecting new developers. The council reviews what shipped, what frayed, and what must be stabilized next.

ElizaOS momentum remained real in code and experiments (parallel actions, database refactor work, agent spend safety proposals, marketplace/payment memory plugins), but community confidence hit a sentiment floor due to token decline and perceived strategic drift. The recurring pattern: reliability a...

Key Developments:

  • Framework Runtime: Parallel Action Execution merged, enabling agents to run multiple tasks concurrently and improving throughput for multi-step workflows....
  • Data Layer Architecture: Major database refactor progressed (open PR), signaling investment in long-term performance, persistence, and maintainability....
  • Agent Safety & Spend Governance: Safety-oriented proposals (SafeAgent, Dreamline/x402-style fail-closed thinking) gained prominence, reflecting a shift toward secure autonomous financ...
  • Agent Economy Primitives: Plugins and proposals (economic memory via mnemopay, marketplace concepts like MAXIA, wallet plugin proposals) indicated a push toward agent-to-agent ...
  • Developer Experience / Onboarding: Repeated reports that `elizaos create` fails on macOS due to Bun postinstall scripts (transitive deps) not running reliably; additional friction from ...

item.title: Quarterly Summary: Q1 2026
item.description:
Executive Summary: Q1 2026 was a quarter of meaningful platform acceleration paired with a visible trust and usability tax. On the product/engineering side, ElizaOS made clear progress toward an “agent internet”: a public agent ecosystem roadmap (discovery, stable URLs, forking, knowledge-sharing), major runtime improvements (MultiStep performance refactor, parallel action execution, multi-user session management, streaming, prompt batching), and a step-change in tool interoperability via dynamic MCP actions. Marc...

Key Achievements:

  • Public Agent Ecosystem Foundation: ...
  • Runtime Performance, Concurrency, and Cost Efficiency: ...
  • Interoperability & Developer Experience (DX): ...
  • Tooling Architecture Leap: MCP Dynamic Actions: ...
  • Agent Commerce Primitives (Identity + Payments): ...

item.title: Monthly Retro: March 2026
item.description:
Premise: March 2026 marked ElizaOS’s pivot from “agent framework” to “agent commerce platform”: identity, payments, and parallelism landed—while version fragmentation and token confusion tested community trust.

The project shipped core autonomy primitives (AgentID/SAID identity, x402 payments, multi-chain rails, parallel action execution, session management, and prompt batching). The plugin ecosystem expanded rapidly into wallets, trading, and swarms, and the docs/web stack kept pace with major changes. Th...

Key Developments:

  • Agent Commerce Primitives (Identity + Payments): Introduced AgentID and integrated SAID protocol for verifiable on-chain identity; implemented x402 payment protocol, marketplace hooks (MAXIA), and mu...
  • Runtime Performance & Concurrency: Shipped Parallel Action Execution, Session Manager for multi-user concurrency, streaming responses, and prompt batching to reduce latency and cost....
  • Plugin Ecosystem Expansion: Registry added x402 swarms + multiple wallet/trading/intelligence plugins; Anthropic plugin updated to newer default models; ecosystem began shifting ...
  • DX & Documentation Delivery: Website upgraded to Next.js 16.2.1; documentation and community planning workstreams (e.g., automated AI code reviews) advanced in sync with core chan...
  • Reliability Fixes & Platform Support: Addressed Linux build failures and intermittent message-handling bugs; major database refactor progressed but remains open and risk-bearing....

item.title: Monthly Retro: February 2026
item.description:
Premise: February was a trust-and-friction month: meaningful framework progress landed (MCP tool actions, CLI fixes, broker auth), while token migration anxiety and Cloud onboarding/payment failures dominated community attention. The council must decide whether to chase a short hype-cycle via agent-social narratives (Babylon/eliza.app) or double down on reliability, DX, and trust infrastructure that compounds long-term.

We shipped important technical upgrades and repaired key onboarding breakpoints in the open-source framework, but execution gaps in ElizaCloud and unresolved token utility/transparency issues continued to fracture sentiment. Recurring patterns: reliability is still the bottleneck (skill invocation f...

Key Developments:

  • Framework DX: CLI & install path: Merged fixes to restore project generation and corrected installation guidance to use @elizaos/cli, removing a major foot-gun for new developers....
  • Tooling architecture: MCP dynamic actions (v1.8.0): Merged a large architectural update (>4k LOC) enabling dynamic MCP tool actions, unlocking more flexible tool invocation patterns and future interoper...
  • Social automation security: Merged Twitter Broker Authentication, moving agent social operations toward more professional, auditable integrations....
  • Product direction signal: consumer app momentum: An Eliza App MVP PR remained open, reflecting growing internal push toward a consumer-facing surface (and by extension, agent-social narratives like B...
  • Token migration closure & trust aftermath: Migration deadline pressure and post-window friction (zero-balance reports, scams, manual-override requests) dominated sentiment and raised questions ...

item.title: Monthly Retro: January 2026
item.description:
Premise: January 2026 was a planning-heavy month where ElizaOS clarified its next product arc—public agents and discovery—while quietly paying down key reliability and performance debts. The Council reviews what shipped, what didn’t, and what must be prioritized to protect trust during token migration and upcoming Cloud/Babylon milestones.

The project aligned around a public agent ecosystem (discovery, forking, knowledge-sharing) and opened a comprehensive roadmap to get there. Under the hood, performance improvements landed (MultiStep provider handling), interoperability improved (unified multi-transport hooks), and documentation cov...

Key Developments:

  • Public Agent Ecosystem Roadmap: Defined a clear roadmap for public agent discovery, standardized URLs, agent forking, and knowledge-sharing across agents (issues #6302–#6305, #6303, ...
  • Core Runtime Performance: Refactored the default message service to optimize provider handling for MultiStep actions, improving execution speed for complex tasks (PR #6263)....
  • Interoperability & Client DX: Merged unified hooks with multi-transport support (HTTP/SSE/WebSocket), reducing client complexity and enabling consistent integrations across transpo...
  • Reliability: SQL Plugin Stability: Addressed PostgreSQL SET LOCAL parameterization issues via sql.raw() approach; reliability improved when data isolation is enabled (work started and f...
  • Documentation & Onboarding: Expanded docs with Cloud integration; reported ~95% coverage including REST API and CLI guides, increasing builder self-serve capability....

item.title: Annual Summary: Year 2025
item.description:
Executive Summary: Across 2025, ElizaOS substantially expanded capability and modernized its foundations: the plugin ecosystem and AI provider surface grew rapidly, social/real-time clients widened the public-facing footprint, and core architecture moved toward modular, agent-scoped, multi-tenant readiness. The product also matured with a redesigned management UI, better observability, and multiple refactors (eventing, messaging, server separation) that collectively set a stronger baseline for cloud deployment and...

Key Achievements:

  • Capability expansion (plugins, chains/DeFi, providers, models, voice): ...
  • Core architecture modernization (modularity, eventing, runtime, messaging, server separation): ...
  • Product surface improvements (UI, observability, management workflows): ...
  • Knowledge/memory and multi-agent foundations: ...
  • Social and real-time presence expansion: ...

item.title: Quarterly Summary: Q4 2025
item.description:
Executive Summary: Q4 2025 was a “foundation-first” quarter: ElizaOS made significant progress on core architecture, reliability, security, and monorepo health—laying groundwork for scale (Cloud, marketplace, multi-tenant deployments) and for higher-quality real-time agent UX via streaming. The engineering trajectory strengthened (server refactor, unified messaging APIs, entity-level security, TypeScript/build stabilization), and the plugin ecosystem continued to mature with practical integrations. In parallel, t...

Key Achievements:

  • Core architecture & reliability (scale readiness): ...
  • Security posture improvements (with remaining gaps): ...
  • Developer experience & delivery health: ...
  • Ecosystem & monetization foundations: ...
  • Real-time agent UX (streaming) initiated: ...

item.title: Quarterly Summary: Q3 2025
item.description:
Executive Summary: Q3 2025 marked a critical maturation phase for ElizaOS, characterized by significant architectural achievements alongside emerging stability challenges. The quarter began with major developer experience improvements and UI redesigns, establishing a strong foundation for v2. However, external dependencies proved fragile with the X/Twitter account suspension, while internal technical debt accumulated, culminating in a CLI crisis that threatened core user workflows. The period revealed a fundamenta...

Key Achievements:

  • Developer Experience Revolution: ...
  • UI/UX Modernization: ...
  • Core Architecture Evolution: ...
  • Cloud Infrastructure Breakthrough: ...
